purpose: Third human-eval batch. Deliberately weighted to OFF-REGISTER dimensions (consumption, sport, economy, culture, history, language) per batch-2 steer, not physical geography/demographics. Filters built to leave real competition; no cardinality reveals, no named comparators, metrics specified. NOT filed to the library.
rules_applied: Off-register dimensions; cap per-capita-consumption at 2, spread across kinds.
Every filter leaves close finalists (no decorative filters that just relabel the obvious giant).
No set-size reveals, no named comparators, superlatives state their metric.
Reflex-defeaters preferred: the obvious first guess is the wrong one.

candidates.q: People in which country drink more beer per person than anywhere else?
candidates.note: Defeats the Germany reflex; Czechia has led for decades. Finalists Czechia, Austria, Germany, Poland.

candidates.q: Which is the most populous country that has never won an Olympic medal of any kind?
candidates.note: As of 2024. Next is Cambodia at ~16M — a tenth the size. Finalists Bangladesh, DR Congo, Myanmar, Nepal.

candidates.q: Some countries have given up their own money and use the US dollar as their official currency. Which of them has the most people?
candidates.note: Ahead of El Salvador and Panama. Finalists Ecuador, El Salvador, Panama, Zimbabwe.

candidates.q: Which country makes more feature-length films each year than any other?
candidates.note: Defeats the Hollywood reflex; Nigeria (Nollywood) is the real #2. Finalists India, Nigeria, USA, China.

candidates.q: Which country produces more olive oil than any other?
candidates.note: ~45-50% of world output — defeats the Italy/Greece reflex. Finalists Spain, Italy, Greece, Tunisia.

candidates.q: Which country has the world's oldest royal family still on the throne, going back many centuries unbroken?
candidates.note: The Imperial House is the oldest continuous hereditary monarchy. Finalists Japan, Denmark, United Kingdom, Morocco.

candidates.q: Which country now ships more cars abroad than any other, counted by number of vehicles?
candidates.note: Overtook Japan in 2023 (4.91M vs 4.42M). Flag: recent, metric = units exported. Finalists China, Japan, Germany, South Korea.

candidates.q: Which country was the first in the world to give women the right to vote in national elections?
candidates.note: Finalists New Zealand, Australia, Finland, Norway.

candidates.q: People in which country drink more tea per person than anywhere else?
candidates.note: Defeats the UK/China reflex by a wide margin. Finalists Turkey, Ireland, United Kingdom, China.

candidates.q: Which country has more living languages spoken within its borders than any other?
candidates.note: World's most linguistically diverse. Finalists Papua New Guinea, Indonesia, Nigeria, India.

candidates.q: Which country's economy leans on money sent home by workers abroad more than any other, as a share of everything it produces?
candidates.note: Tonga (~41%) and Samoa are close. Flag: 2023 figures. Finalists Tajikistan, Tonga, Samoa, Lebanon.

candidates.q: Which country runs the world's oldest central bank still in operation today?
candidates.note: The Riksbank, founded 1668; the Bank of England (1694) is the close runner-up. Finalists Sweden, United Kingdom, Netherlands, France.
